editorial.title: Xi emboldens Kim Jong-un
editorial.body: Kim Jong-un has good reason to believe that time is on his side. North Korea’s restoration of a mutual defense alliance with Russia in 2024 appears...
editorial.pullQuote: Kim Jong-un has good reason to believe that time is on his side.
summary: Kim Jong-un gains confidence as North Korea rebuilds its Russian alliance in 2024.
Xi's support emboldens Kim to pursue aggressive regional strategies.
The China-Russia-North Korea axis reshapes Northeast Asian power dynamics.
editorial.stance: Growing authoritarian alignment threatens regional stability and international order.
summaryTr: 김정은이 2024년 북한의 러시아 동맹 재건으로 자신감을 얻다.
시진핑의 지지가 김정은을 공격적 지역 전략 추진으로 강하게 부추기다.
중국-러시아-북한 축이 동북아시아 힘의 역학관계를 재편하다.
editorial.bodyTr: 김정은은 시간이 자신의 편이라고 믿을 충분한 이유가 있다. 2024년 북한이 러시아와 상호 방위 동맹을 복원한 것으로 보인다...

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: Editorial frames China-Russia-North Korea alignment as a threat to 'international order' and emphasizes security concerns rather than advocating dialogue or questioning alliance costs, reflecting conservative geopolitical framing.

editorial.title: '하후상박' 기초연금 개편 논의, 합리적 기준 도출을
editorial.body: (사설) 정부가 기초연금 개편에 착수해 노인 빈곤 해소와 제도 지속가능성을 모색한다. 저소득 노인 지원 강화, 하후상박 구조 개편, 기초연금 지급 기준 조정 등이 논의 중이다. |
summary: 정부가 노인 빈곤 해소를 위해 기초연금 개편에 착수했다.
저소득 노인 지원 강화와 하후상박 구조 개편이 주요 과제다.
제도 지속가능성과 형평성을 모두 고려한 합리적 기준이 필요하다.
editorial.pullQuote: 저소득 노인 지원 강화, 하후상박 구조 개편, 기초연금 지급 기준 조정 등이 논의 중이다.
editorial.stance: 노인 빈곤 해소와 제도 지속가능성을 균형있게 고려한 객관적 기준 도출을 주문한다.
editorial.titleTr: Debate over "lower the rich, raise the poor" basic pension reform seeks rational criteria
summaryTr: The government has begun reforming the basic pension system to address elderly poverty.
Strengthening support for low-income seniors and restructuring the progressive system are key priorities.
Rational standards that consider both sustainability and fairness of the system are needed.
editorial.bodyTr: The government is moving forward with reforms to the basic pension system, seeking to address elderly poverty and ensure the system's sustainability. Under discussion are strengthened support for low-income seniors, restructuring of the progressive benefit system, and adjustments to basic pension eligibility criteria.

leanScore.label: Center

editorial.title: [사설] 선거 이긴 당에서 내부 충돌, 민주당도 윤 정권 닮아가나
editorial.body: 선거 이긴 당에서 내부 충돌, 민주당도 윤 정권 닮아가나 민주당 친이재명계와 친정청래계가 10일 지방선거 책임론을 놓고 충돌했다. 친명계 모임인 더민주혁신회의는 이길 선거를 놓친 지도부는 백의종군으로 책임지라며 정 대표 사퇴를 요구했다. 이 대통령의 복심이라는
editorial.pullQuote: 이길 선거를 놓친 지도부는 백의종군으로 책임지라며 정 대표 사퇴를 요구했다.
summary: 민주당 내부에서 친이재명계와 친정청래계가 지방선거 책임론으로 충돌했다.
친명계는 선거 패배 책임을 물으며 정 대표의 사퇴를 요구했다.
선거 승리한 당에서 내부 갈등이 심화되고 있다.
editorial.stance: 선거 승리 이후 당내 권력 투쟁이 심화되는 현상을 비판하며 결집과 성찰을 촉구한다.
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Winning Party Torn by Internal Conflict—Is the Democratic Party Becoming Like the Yoon Administration?
summaryTr: Internal divisions within the Democratic Party intensified between the pro-Lee Jae-myung faction and the pro-Jung Chul-ran faction over responsibility for the local elections.
The pro-Lee faction demanded the resignation of Jung, the party leader, holding him accountable for the election defeat.
Internal conflict is deepening within the party that won the election.
editorial.bodyTr:
Translation

The ruling party embroiled in internal conflict — is the Democratic Party becoming like the Yoon administration?

The Democratic Party's pro-Lee Jae-myung faction and pro-Jung Chul-ran faction clashed on the 10th over responsibility for the local elections. The More Democratic Innovation Council, a pro-Lee group, demanded that Party Chairman Jung step down, saying the leadership that lost a winnable election should take responsibility by withdrawing from politics. editorials.titleTr: Editorial: New PPP Floor Leader Must Heed Public Demand to Break with Yoon and Pursue Complete Reform
summaryTr: Rep. Jung Chung-sik won the runoff vote to become the new floor leader of the People Power Party, winning by a margin of 7 votes.
His selection came after a tight race, contrary to expectations that the pro-Yoon faction would secure over half the votes in the first round.
Public sentiment is demanding a complete break with Yoon Suk-yeol and a sweeping overhaul of the party.
editorials.bodyTr: The ruling Democratic Party's new floor leader should listen to the people's call to break with Yoon and undergo wholesale reform. Rep. Jung Chung-sik, a three-term lawmaker, was elected as the new floor leader of the People Power Party. While it was known that he had the backing of pro-Yoon factions and predictions suggested he would win by majority vote in the first round, he ultimately prevailed by a margin of 7 votes in a runoff. editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Democracy Declaration from College Students Emerges 39 Years After Democratization
summaryTr: Student councils from 18 universities condemned the shortage of ballots in the local elections.
University students issued a statement on democracy for the first time in 39 years on the commemoration day of democratization.
On the 39th anniversary of the June 10 Democratic Struggle, they raised their voices for democracy.
editorials.bodyTr:
Thirty-nine Years After Democratization, University Students Release Democracy Statement on Current Situation

Student councils from 18 universities nationwide held a protest on the 10th condemning the shortage of ballots in the June 3rd local elections and released a statement on the current political situation. [연합뉴스] 전시작전통제권(전작권) 전환을 위한 한·미 양국의 평가 수준을 공개한 안규백 국방부 장관의 발언이 비밀 유출 논란에 휩싸였다. 안 장관은 지난달 31일 싱가포르 출장 중 “한·미 양국은 2020년에 전작권 전환 조건의 94%가 이미 충족됐다고 합의한 것을 비롯해 우리의 능력에 대해 충분히 설명했다”고 밝혔다. 이날 아시아안보회의(샹그릴라 대화)에 참석한 미국 의원들을 만나 한국군의 전작권 행사 능력을 설명하고 협조를 요청했다는 것이다. 한·미는 조건이 갖춰지면 전작권을 한국군에 이양하기로 하고, 구체적인 매뉴얼을 만들어 평가를 진행 중이다. 하지만 구체적인 평가 항목이나 이행 수준은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 간주하고 있다. 자칫 한·미 연합 전력 수준과 방어 태세가 북한 등에 공개될 것을 우려해서다. 그런데 국방부 수장이 나서 양국 군 당국이 공개를 꺼려 온 94%라는 구체적인 수치를 언급한 것이다. 논란이 일자 국방부는 “조속한 전작권 회복과 관련한 국민의 알권리 보장과 이해를 돕기 위해 개략적인 수치를 제시한 것”이라며 “구체적인 내용을 적시한 게 아니어서 한·미 연합 비밀에 해당하지 않는다”고 해명했다. 하지만 국방부는 지난해 전작권 전환 평가 결과에 대한 국회의 자료 제출 요구에 “(전작권과 관련한) 평가 항목 및 결과 등 구체적인 사항은 한·미 연합의 비밀로 답변이 제한된다”며 비공개 입장을 고수한 바 있다. 전작권 평가 추정이 가능한 자료를 공개하지 않겠다는 뜻이다. 군 당국이 지금까지 7년 넘게 국회에 관련 내용을 설명하지 않은 것은 사안의 민감성을 방증한다. 미국은 지난 3월 정동영 통일부 장관의 북한 구성시 우라늄 농축시설 존재 언급을 이유로 아직까지 북핵 동향 관련 정보 제공을 중단하고 있다. 한·미 동맹에 균열 징후가 이어지고 있는 만큼 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다. 한·미는 어제부터 1박2일 동안 한국군의 핵추진잠수함 보유와 우라늄 농축 등과 관련한 협의를 시작했다. 현 정부가 역점을 두고 있는 전작권 환수나 핵추진잠수함 보유 등은 결국 한·미 동맹의 튼튼한 신뢰가 기반이 돼야 순조롭게 진행될 수 있다는 점을 분명히 인식할 필요가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 책임 있는 당국자라면 단어 하나에도 신중을 기해 발언하는 것이 맞다.
summary: 국방장관이 전작권 전환 평가 수준 94%를 공개해 비밀 유출 논란 초래했다.
군 당국은 한·미 연합 비밀이라며 국회에 자료 제출을 거부해왔다.
한·미 동맹 신뢰가 기반되어야 전작권 환수 등이 순조롭게 진행된다.
editorial.stance: 정부는 민감한 안보 사안에서 일관성 있고 신중한 정보 관리로 한·미 신뢰를 지켜야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Defense Minister's Remarks Invite Classified Information Leak Controversy
summaryTr: The defense minister disclosed a 94% readiness assessment level for the transition of wartime operational control, triggering controversy over secret information leaks.
Military authorities have refused to submit documents to the National Assembly, citing the information as a South Korea-U.S. alliance secret.
The smooth progress of regaining wartime operational control depends on building trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.
editorial.bodyTr:
Defense Minister An Kyou-paik has become embroiled in a controversy over leaked classified information after publicly disclosing the assessment level between South Korea and the United States regarding the transfer of operational control. During a business trip to Singapore last month, Minister An stated, "South Korea and the United States agreed that 94 percent of the conditions for the transfer of wartime operational control (OPCON) had already been met as of 2020, and we have sufficiently explained our capabilities." He reportedly met with U.S. lawmakers attending the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore to brief them on the South Korean military's ability to exercise operational control and to request cooperation.

Seoul and Washington have agreed to transfer operational control to the South Korean military once conditions are met, and are currently conducting assessments based on detailed manuals. However, the specific evaluation criteria and implementation levels are classified as a South Korea-U.S. joint military secret due to concerns that operational capability levels and defense postures could be exposed to North Korea and other adversaries.

Yet the head of the Defense Ministry publicly mentioned the specific figure of 94 percent, which military authorities on both sides have been reluctant to disclose. When controversy ensued, the Defense Ministry explained that it had presented only a rough figure to guarantee public awareness and understanding regarding the early recovery of operational control, and that it did not mention specific details and therefore did not constitute a breach of South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. However, the ministry previously maintained a position of non-disclosure when rejecting the National Assembly's request for materials regarding last year's OPCON transfer assessment results, stating that responses were restricted as evaluation criteria and results constituted South Korea-U.S. joint military secrets. This amounts to refusing to disclose information from which OPCON assessments could be inferred.

The military authorities' refusal to explain related matters to the National Assembly for over seven years demonstrates the sensitivity of the issue. The United States has suspended the provision of information related to North Korean nuclear developments since March, citing Unification Minister Chung Dong-young's remarks about the existence of uranium enrichment facilities in a unified Korean peninsula. Given the continuing signs of fissures in the South Korea-U.S. alliance, responsible government officials must exercise caution in every word they speak. Starting yesterday, South Korea and the United States began one-and-a-half-day consultations regarding the South Korean military's acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines and uranium enrichment. It must be clearly recognized that priority initiatives of the current government—such as the recovery of operational control and the acquisition of nuclear-powered submarines—can only proceed smoothly when built on a strong foundation of trust in the South Korea-U.S. alliance.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 국방장관의 비밀 유출을 비판하며 한·미 동맹의 신뢰와 안보 체계 유지를 강조하는 관료적·보수적 입장으로, 정부 신뢰성과 한·미 연합 기밀 유지를 핵심 프레임으로 삼음

editorial.title: Predetermined verdict
editorial.body: Lee signals intent to enact special counsel bill allowing withdrawal of indictments At a press conference marking the first anniversary of his inauguration on Monday, President Lee Jae Myung said there was no choice but to pursue the "special counsel bill on manipulated indictments." He added, "I could direct the prosecution and police to establish a large-scale special investigative task force. That would be the normal and customary course of action." He spoke as though he had made a concession to the opposition and public opinion by agreeing to the special counsel bill on manipulated indictments rather than establishing a special investigative task force. That claim is absurd. The bill's central aim is to review whether the prosecution's investigations and indictments were justified in roughly a dozen criminal cases involving Lee and several of his key aides who were brought to trial. Prior to the June 3 local elections, Lee told the ruling party to "solicit opinions and engage in further deliberation" on the bill. He has now signaled that he intends to put the special counsel bill into effect if the National Assembly passes it. A key point is that, unlike previous special counsels, the special counsel to be appointed under the controversial bill proposed by the Democratic Party of Korea would have the authority to withdraw indictments. This means that if the prosecution's investigation and indictment of Lee are found to have been improper, the special counsel could, on its own authority, withdraw the charges. Amid growing backlash against the provision granting the special counsel the authority to withdraw indictments, the Democratic Party has postponed action on the bill until after the June 3 elections. At a Cabinet meeting on June 2, the day before the elections, Lee told acting Prosecutor General Ku Ja-hyeon, "Anyone can make mistakes. If a mistake has been made, it should be apologized for and withdrawn." He added, "The prosecution wields enormous power and should bear responsibility commensurate with that power." The president's remarks seem to suggest that he regards the alleged manipulation as established fact and that the implementation of the special counsel law is therefore a natural consequence. Given its track record of repeatedly pushing trough legislation unilaterally, there is a strong likelihood that the Democratic Party will sideline the opposition and push through the bill. The party holds 161 of the Assembly's 300 seats — an outright majority — and Speaker Cho Jeong-sik is a six-term lawmaker from the party. Earlier, a National Assembly inquiry spearheaded by the Democratic Party concluded that "both the investigation and the indictment had been manipulated." This is tantamount to sending an advance signal that "we have already decided what the final conclusion should be, and the special counsel need only conduct the investigation accordingly and reach the same conclusion." However, the claim that the investigation and indictment were manipulated have not been substantiated either through a National Assembly inquiry or through an internal prosecutorial inspection. Nevertheless, the president has signaled his intention to enforce a law that could lead to the cancellation of the charges against him, while the ruling party continues to advance what critics regard as a baseless claim. The public is unlikely to have confidence in the neutrality and fairness of any special counsel investigation conducted under this bill. Analysts say that the formation of a group led by pro-Lee lawmakers advocating the withdrawal of indictments, together with their push for the special counsel bill, adversely affected public sentiment toward the Democratic Party and its results in the local elections. The opposition candidate was elected mayor of Seoul in what was considered the most important race of the elections. If the ruling party steamrolls the bill through, it will face an even stronger backlash in the next election. If the party and Lee are genuinely seeking to uncover the truth about the alleged manipulated indictments, the provision empowering the special counsel to withdraw indictments should be removed from the bill. summary: President Lee signals intent to enact special counsel bill allowing withdrawal of indictments against him.
The Democratic Party majority has predetermined the investigation's conclusion without substantiated evidence of misconduct.
Critics warn the ruling party risks severe electoral backlash if it steamrolls the controversial bill through.
editorial.stance: Editorial opposes the special counsel bill as a predetermined political maneuver to overturn unjustified charges against President Lee.
summaryTr: 이 대통령, 자신에 대한 기소 취소를 허용하는 특별검사법 제정 의사 시사
민주당 다수파, 위법 행위의 입증된 증거 없이 조사 결론을 미리 정해놓았다
야권 인사들, 논란이 많은 법안을 강행하면 여당이 심각한 선거 역풍을 맞을 수 있다고 경고
editorial.bodyTr: 이준석 대통령이 월요일 취임 1주년 기자회견에서 "조작 기소 특별검사법"을 추진할 수밖에 없다고 말했다. 그는 "검찰과 경찰에 대규모 특별수사단을 구성하도록 지시할 수 있다. 그것이 정상적이고 관례적인 조치"라고 덧붙였다. 그는 대규모 특별수사단을 구성하는 대신 조작 기소 특별검사법에 동의함으로써 야당과 여론에 양보했다는 식으로 말했다. 이러한 주장은 터무니없다. 이 법안의 핵심은 이 대통령과 재판에 넘겨진 그의 핵심 측근들과 관련된 약 12개의 형사 사건에서 검찰의 수사와 기소가 정당했는지 여부를 검토하는 것이다. 6월 3일 지방선거 이전에 이 대통령은 여당에 "의견 수렴과 추가 검토"를 하도록 지시했다. 그는 이제 국회가 이 법안을 통과시키면 특별검사법을 시행할 의도를 시사했다. 핵심은 기존 특별검사와 달리 민주당이 제안한 논란이 많은 법안에 따라 임명될 특별검사가 기소를 취소할 권한을 갖는다는 점이다. 이는 검찰의 이 대통령에 대한 수사와 기소가 부당한 것으로 판단되면 특별검사가 자신의 권한으로 혐의를 취소할 수 있다는 의미다. 특별검사의 기소 취소 권한에 대한 반발이 커지면서 민주당은 6월 3일 선거 이후로 이 법안의 처리를 미뤘다. 선거 전날인 6월 2일 국무회의에서 이 대통령은 구자혁 검찰총장 직무대행에게 "누구든 실수할 수 있다. 실수가 있었다면 사과하고 철회해야 한다"고 말했다. 그는 "검찰은 막강한 권력을 가지고 있고 그에 상응하는 책임을 져야 한다"고 덧붙였다. 대통령의 발언은 그가 조작 의혹을 확정된 사실로 보고 있으며 특별검사법 시행이 따라서 자연스러운 결과라고 생각한다는 의도로 보인다. 법안을 일방적으로 강행해 온 전력이 있는 민주당이 야당을 제쳐두고 이 법안을 통과시킬 가능성이 높다. 민주당은 국회 300석 중 161석으로 절대다수를 차지하고 있으며 조정석 의장은 민주당 6선 의원이다. 앞서 민주당이 주도한 국회 청문회는 "수사와 기소 모두 조작됐다"고 결론 내렸다. 이는 "우리는 이미 최종 결론을 정했으니 특별검사는 그에 따라 수사하고 같은 결론을 내리면 된다"는 신호를 미리 보내는 것과 같다. 그러나 수사와 기소가 조작됐다는 주장은 국회 청문회나 검찰 자체 감시를 통해서도 입증되지 않았다. 그럼에도 불구하고 대통령은 자신에 대한 혐의를 취소할 수 있는 법안을 시행할 의도를 시사했고, 여당은 근거 없다는 비판을 받고 있는 주장을 계속 제기하고 있다. 이 법안에 따라 진행되는 특별검사 수사의 중립성과 공정성에 대해 국민이 신뢰를 갖기는 어려울 것이다. 분석가들은 이준석 지지 의원들로 구성된 기소 취소 옹호 집단의 구성과 특별검사법 추진이 민주당에 대한 여론을 악화시키고 지방선거 결과에 영향을 미쳤다고 지적한다. 가장 중요한 선거로 평가받는 서울시장 선거에서 야당 후보가 당선됐다. 여당이 법안을 일방적으로 통과시키면 다음 선거에서 더욱 강한 반발에 직면할 것이다. 당과 이 대통령이 진정으로 조작 기소 의혹에 대한 진실을 규명하려 한다면, 특별검사에게 기소를 취소할 권한을 부여하는 조항을 법안에서 삭제해야 한다.

editorials.title: Predetermined verdict
editorials.body: Lee signals intent to enact special counsel bill allowing withdrawal of indictments At a press conference marking the first anniversary of his inauguration on Monday, President Lee Jae Myung said there was no choice but to pursue the "special counsel bill on manipulated indictments." He added, "I could direct the prosecution and police to establish a large-scale special investigative task force. That would be the normal and customary course of action." He spoke as though he had made a concession to the opposition and public opinion by agreeing to the special counsel bill on manipulated indictments rather than establishing a special investigative task force. That claim is absurd. The bill's central aim is to review whether the prosecution's investigations and indictments were justified in roughly a dozen criminal cases involving Lee and several of his key aides who were brought to trial. Prior to the June 3 local elections, Lee told the ruling party to "solicit opinions and engage in further deliberation" on the bill. He has now signaled that he intends to put the special counsel bill into effect if the National Assembly passes it. A key point is that, unlike previous special counsels, the special counsel to be appointed under the controversial bill proposed by the Democratic Party of Korea would have the authority to withdraw indictments. This means that if the prosecution's investigation and indictment of Lee are found to have been improper, the special counsel could, on its own authority, withdraw the charges. Amid growing backlash against the provision granting the special counsel the authority to withdraw indictments, the Democratic Party has postponed action on the bill until after the June 3 elections. At a Cabinet meeting on June 2, the day before the elections, Lee told acting Prosecutor General Ku Ja-hyeon, "Anyone can make mistakes. If a mistake has been made, it should be apologized for and withdrawn." He added, "The prosecution wields enormous power and should bear responsibility commensurate with that power." The president's remarks seem to suggest that he regards the alleged manipulation as established fact and that the implementation of the special counsel law is therefore a natural consequence. Given its track record of repeatedly pushing trough legislation unilaterally, there is a strong likelihood that the Democratic Party will sideline the opposition and push through the bill. The party holds 161 of the Assembly's 300 seats — an outright majority — and Speaker Cho Jeong-sik is a six-term lawmaker from the party. Earlier, a National Assembly inquiry spearheaded by the Democratic Party concluded that "both the investigation and the indictment had been manipulated." This is tantamount to sending an advance signal that "we have already decided what the final conclusion should be, and the special counsel need only conduct the investigation accordingly and reach the same conclusion." However, the claim that the investigation and indictment were manipulated have not been substantiated either through a National Assembly inquiry or through an internal prosecutorial inspection. Nevertheless, the president has signaled his intention to enforce a law that could lead to the cancellation of the charges against him, while the ruling party continues to advance what critics regard as a baseless claim. The public is unlikely to have confidence in the neutrality and fairness of any special counsel investigation conducted under this bill. Analysts say that the formation of a group led by pro-Lee lawmakers advocating the withdrawal of indictments, together with their push for the special counsel bill, adversely affected public sentiment toward the Democratic Party and its results in the local elections. The opposition candidate was elected mayor of Seoul in what was considered the most important race of the elections. If the ruling party steamrolls the bill through, it will face an even stronger backlash in the next election. If the party and Lee are genuinely seeking to uncover the truth about the alleged manipulated indictments, the provision empowering the special counsel to withdraw indictments should be removed from the bill. The Democratic Party majority has predetermined the investigation's conclusion without substantiated evidence of misconduct.
Critics warn the ruling party risks severe electoral backlash if it steamrolls the controversial bill through.
editorials.stance: Editorial opposes the special counsel bill as a predetermined political maneuver to overturn unjustified charges against President Lee.

당의 변화 조짐이 있으나 극단주의 극복이 과제다.
editorial.bodyTr: Jung Kyeong-suk, a three-term lawmaker, was elected as the new floor leader of the People Power Party. A former prosecutor classified as part of the "pro-Yoon Seok-yeol faction" within the party, his appointment may disappoint those who hoped the party would draw a clear line from those who condone insurrection. However, there were signs that suggested the People Power Party could undergo meaningful change. Extreme voices both inside and outside the party were being pushed to the margins, and there were indications that a more moderate direction might take root.

당의 변화 조짐이 있으나 극단주의 극복이 과제다.
editorials.bodyTr: Jung Kyeong-suk, a three-term lawmaker, was elected as the new floor leader of the People Power Party. A former prosecutor classified as part of the "pro-Yoon Seok-yeol faction" within the party, his appointment may disappoint those who hoped the party would draw a clear line from those who condone insurrection. However, there were signs that suggested the People Power Party could undergo meaningful change. Extreme voices both inside and outside the party were being pushed to the margins, and there were indications that a more moderate direction might take root.

editorial.titleTr: Seoul Apartment Median Price Hits Record High as Middle Class Struggles with Homeownership
summaryTr: The median price of Seoul apartments has reached an all-time high of 1.238 billion won.
As of May this year, prices surged 10.56 percent from the beginning of the year, marking the largest increase.
The situation is worsening, with homeownership becoming increasingly difficult even for the middle class.
editorial.bodyTr: It is becoming increasingly difficult for ordinary people in Seoul to purchase a single apartment. According to KB Real Estate statistics, the median price of apartment sales in Seoul in May of this year reached 1.238 billion won, surging 118.33 million won (10.56%) compared to the beginning of the year. This marks the highest level since related statistics began being compiled, and represents the largest increase for the same period on record. editorials.bodyTr: When Rep. Jung Chung-sik, classified as part of the "pro-party establishment" faction, was elected as the new floor leader of the People Power Party on the 10th, criticism poured in from the anti-establishment faction, calling it a return to being "pro-Yoon's party," according to reports.
